name: Dashboard Card Component
description: Dashboard card with loader, multiple states (empty, loading, populated, error), and data display. Follows state-based decomposition pattern.

steps.name: Create TypeScript types
steps.input_template: Feature requirements define card data structure
steps.output_template: Types defined in {{types_path}} or inline in component file

steps.name: Create loader function
steps.action_template: Create load{{DataName}} function fetching card data

steps.name: Create card skeleton
steps.action_template: Create {{CardName}} component skeleton with prop types
steps.output_template: Component file at {{components_path}}/{{card_name}}.tsx with basic structure

steps.name: Implement empty state
steps.action_template: Add empty state UI when no data exists
steps.output_template: Card shows empty state with CTA when data is null/empty

steps.name: Implement loading state
steps.action_template: Add loading skeleton UI using Suspense
steps.output_template: Card shows skeleton loader during data fetch

steps.name: Implement error state
steps.action_template: Add error handling with retry capability
steps.output_template: Card shows error message with retry button on fetch failure

steps.name: Wire to dashboard page
steps.action_template: Add {{CardName}} to dashboard with Suspense boundary
steps.output_template: Card integrated at {{page_path}} with loader data

adaptation_notes: Replace {{CardName}} with PascalCase component name (e.g., CoachingSessionsCard)
Replace {{DataName}} with the data type name (e.g., CoachingSession)
Adjust {{data_fields}} based on what the card displays
For simple cards without external data, skip step 2
Steps 4, 5, 6 can run in parallel as they're independent UI variants
